body:
The short flag '-l' means different things in different subcommands, which causes user/agent confusion when switching:

  gittoc list  -l bug      # label filter
  gittoc new   -l bug      # label
  gittoc update-l bug      # add label
  gittoc show  T-1 -l 5    # LIMIT of notes/events — totally different

See cli.py show_parser (-l/--limit, ~line 398) vs list_parser (-l/--label, ~line 212) etc. Suggested resolution: rename show's limit to '-n/--limit' (matches log's proposed -n and 'show -n' already means --notes — so perhaps '-N/--limit' or drop the short form entirely). Alternatively make '-l' mean label consistently and introduce a new short flag for limit.
title: '-l' flag is overloaded: label in list/new/update, limit in show
